description_long: This house features a steeply pitched, reddish-brown shingle roof and a half-timbered facade with light wooden beams and infill panels. The front elevation is distinguished by two prominent bay windows with polygonal shapes and a small wooden door set to the right, accessed by a set of concrete steps. The lower portion of the house is clad in stone, providing a sturdy base and visual contrast to the lighter upper walls. The windows are symmetrically arranged on the front, while the sides and rear have a more uniform grid of timber framing with no visible windows. The overall design evokes a traditional European or Tudor-style residence, with a compact, rectangular footprint and a single main entrance. The structure appears solid and well-built, suitable for residential use in a historical or themed urban setting.
description: A traditional half-timbered house with a steep shingle roof, stone base, and two front bay windows. The main entrance is accessed by concrete steps and the facade features symmetrical timber framing.
description_view_0: Front view showing two bay windows, the main door, and concrete steps leading up to the entrance.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the half-timbered wall pattern and stone base, with no windows or doors visible.
description_view_2: Rear view with a uniform grid of timber framing and stone base, lacking windows or entry points.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the timber grid and stone base, with a partial view of the front steps.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with prominent front bay windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a small wooden door accessed by concrete steps, with no porch or overhang, and is offset to the right of the facade.
